canonical_definition: The Trauma-Informed Dialogue for Empathy (TIDE) dataset, a resource of 10,000 two-turn dialogues based on 500 diverse, clinically-grounded PTSD personas, used for training and evaluating empathetic response generation.
papers: UnifiedVisual: A Framework for Constructing Unified Vision-Language Datasets

definitions.text: The Trauma-Informed Dialogue for Empathy (TIDE) dataset, a resource of 10,000 two-turn dialogues based on 500 diverse, clinically-grounded PTSD personas, used for training and evaluating empathetic response generation.
definitions.paper: UnifiedVisual: A Framework for Constructing Unified Vision-Language Datasets

definitions.text: A fully synthetic dataset created for research on empathetic AI, containing 10,000 instances derived from 500 client personas designed for PTSD dialogue support evaluation.

characterization: The Trauma-Informed Dialogue for Empathy (TIDE) dataset, a resource of 10,000 two-turn dialogues based on 500 diverse, clinically-grounded PTSD personas, used for training and evaluating empathetic response generation.

emnlp25: UnifiedVisual: A Framework for Constructing Unified Vision-Language Datasets
